Page 1 - Circular on Periodic Compliance Review
===============================================

Circular No. OF-COMP/2026/041

This circular sets out the periodic compliance review process for regulated business units.
Every department shall maintain evidence of compliance with applicable obligations and make
such evidence available to the compliance team during scheduled reviews.

The purpose of this circular is to ensure consistent documentation, accountability, and
traceability of regulatory obligations. This circular does not replace any statutory
obligation imposed by a regulator, exchange, authority, or court.




Page 2 - Definitions
====================

For the purpose of this circular, "business unit" means any internal department, product team,
operating vertical, or regional office responsible for performing regulated activities.

"Compliance evidence" means policies, logs, approvals, screenshots, filings, reports,
reconciliations, confirmations, or other records that demonstrate adherence to applicable
requirements.

"Control owner" means the person responsible for maintaining, operating, and evidencing a
compliance control.




Page 3 - Responsibility of Control Owners
=========================================

Each control owner shall review assigned obligations at least once per quarter. The review
must confirm whether the control remains active, whether supporting records are complete,
and whether any exception has been identified.

Where a control is no longer applicable, the control owner shall provide a written explanation
and obtain approval from the compliance team before retiring the control. The compliance team
may request additional evidence where the explanation is incomplete.




Page 4 - Record Retention
=========================

All compliance evidence must be retained for a minimum period of eight years unless a longer
retention period is required by applicable law, contract, regulatory direction, litigation hold,
or internal policy.

Records may be retained in electronic form if they are complete, accurate, searchable, and
protected against unauthorized alteration. Archived records must remain retrievable within a
reasonable time when requested by audit, compliance, risk, or legal teams.




Page 5 - Exception Reporting
============================

Any failure to perform a control, maintain evidence, or submit a required report must be
recorded as an exception. The exception report should include the affected obligation, date
of occurrence, root cause, remediation owner, target closure date, and supporting evidence.

High-risk exceptions must be escalated to the Head of Compliance within two business days.
Repeated low-risk exceptions may be aggregated and treated as a high-risk exception.




Page 6 - Amendments and Supersession
====================================

This circular supersedes any earlier internal circular dealing exclusively with quarterly
compliance evidence collection. Existing regulatory obligations, customer commitments, and
board-approved policies continue to apply unless expressly amended through an approved policy
document.

The compliance team may amend this circular from time to time. Amendments must be documented
and communicated to affected control owners.




Page 7 - Effective Date
=======================

This circular is effective from 1 May 2026. Business units shall complete the first review
under this circular by 30 June 2026.

Questions regarding interpretation of this circular should be sent to the compliance team.
Control owners remain responsible for implementation until a written clarification is issued.
